meta.status: scaffold — populate via AI research run

models.name: Charitable Company Limited by Guarantee
keyFeatures: Full charity status — gift aid, public benefit
Can have trading subsidiary for commercial income
Eligible for most heritage and community grants
models.niCaseStudy: TODO: Named NI charity using this model

models.name: Community Interest Company (CIC)
keyFeatures: Asset lock — assets cannot be distributed to private benefit

models.name: Development Trust (Community Benefit Society)
cons: Less common in NI heritage sector
models.suitabilityForFernhill: MEDIUM — consider for community investment tranche

models.name: Hybrid: Charity Parent + CIC Trading Subsidiary
keyFeatures: Charity holds the building and receives grants/donations
CIC or Ltd Company runs commercial operations (weddings, wakes, tea room)
Clean separation of charitable and commercial activity
pros: Standard model for UK heritage venues
models.niCaseStudy: TODO: Named NI venue using this hybrid

recommendation.primaryStructure: Charitable Company Limited by Guarantee (CCNI registered)
recommendation.tradingSubsidiary: Private Limited Company wholly owned by the charity
rationale: Maximises grant eligibility across all major heritage funders
Enables gift aid on all donations
Commercial operations ring-fenced in subsidiary — protects charitable status
Standard model trusted by funders (NLHF, AHF, HED NI)
nextSteps: Identify 3–5 founding trustees with heritage, legal, financial, community expertise
Register with CCNI and Companies House
Establish trading subsidiary once charitable registration complete
